titleStmt.title: SII 11.40: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 40. (B.K. No. 113 of 1929-30). NARSALGI, BAGEVADI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE FRONT WALL OF THE HANUMĀN TEMPLE.

summary.p: This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Akālavarshadēva (i.e., Kṛishṇa III) and is dated in Śaka 886, Raktākshin, Phālguṇa, solar eclipse (= Monday, 6th March, A.D. 965). It mentions the Mahāsāmantādhipati Āhavamalla Tailaparasa of the Satyāśraya family as a subordinate of the king, governing Tarddavāḍi One Thousand as aṇuṁgajīvita. He is also given the biruda Chāḷukya-Rāma. A subordinate of his, claiming descent from the Khachara-kula is mentioned but his name is unfortunately lost. The record seems to register a gift of taxes to some god at Narasalage included in the Murttage Thirty division.
This is the first known inscription that establishes the political relationship of the Rāshṭrakṭūas and the Western Chāḷukyas of Kalyāṇi before the latter obtained sovereignty in A.D. 973.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastyakāḷavarṣadēva śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara
mabhaṭṭāraka ruttarōttarābhivṛddhi pravarddhamāna vijayarājyōdayamā candrārkkaṁ
saluttamire tatpādapadmōpajīvi samadhigatapañcamahāśabda mahāsāmantādhi
pati cāḷukyarāma nāhavamalla marāghavaṁ sa
tyāśrayakulatilaka śrīmat tailaparasar tarddavāḍi
sāsiramuma naṇuṅgajīvitamāḷutta sakavarṣa 886 neya raktākṣi saṁvatsa raṁ pra
varttise tadvarṣābhyantarada phālguṇamāsada sūryyagrahaṇadoḷ śrīmat tailapa
vinirggata khacarakulāmala
di vāstavya śrīmannā murttage mūvatta ṟoḷagaṇa
lageya
maṁ nāḻcaṭṭa poṅga
dyāṇamaydu perggaḍe gadyāṇa mai kiṟudeṟe daṇḍāya meṁbivu modalāge teṟa
ma mondumilladavarggamādu ruva gadyāṇa sahaṇikāṭi kūḻu
tuppa māna mondu mosa raḍu dēvara vaṇigeyakūḻu
varjja Intī sthitiyaṁ tappa salla tappidāta vāraṇāsiyoḷ Uttarāyaṇasa
ḷ pannirbbar cauvērarumaṁ peṇḍaraṁ kavileyumanaṟida mahāpātakamā
dante yatte purāṇa svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasundharāṁ
srāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē ṣvatōyā ṭaravāsinaḥ
ntē brahmadēyā sudhā bhuktā
bhūmistasya tasya tadā phaḷa . There appear to be a line or two after this, much damaged.
Digital edition of SII 11.40 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
